description: Unique synthetic transaction identifier (TX######).
Date the transaction occurred.
Date the transaction posted/settled.
notes: Usually same day or within 1–2 days.

description: Helper field for monthly aggregation.
Synthetic account name.
notes: Checking, savings, credit card, or brokerage.

description: One of checking, savings, credit_card, brokerage.
Normalized merchant name.
Normalized merchant key used in the labeled file.
Noisy bank-export-style transaction text.
notes: Use this for keyword mapping tests.

description: Signed USD amount. Positive = inflow, negative = outflow.
notes: This is the main numeric field.

description: Transaction currency.
notes: Always USD in this synthetic set.

description: Payment channel such as Card, ACH, Autopay, Transfer, Debit, Direct Deposit, Brokerage.
Merchant city.
notes: Optional helper for future location-based features.

description: Merchant state.
notes: Always CA or NY/WA/other U.S. state when relevant.

description: income, expense, transfer, refund, investment_buy, investment_income.
Top-level category.
More specific category.
green = essential/committed, red = discretionary/flexible.
Whether the transaction is part of a repeating series.
Whether the repeating series behaves like a subscription/recurring service.
Stable group ID for repeated merchants/services.
notes: Key field for recurring and price-change detection.

description: monthly, biweekly, or blank for non-recurring transactions.
Sequence number within a recurring group.
Days since the previous transaction in the same recurring group.
Prior signed amount in the same recurring group.
True when the recurring amount changed vs. the prior occurrence.
Difference between current and prior signed recurring amount.
notes: Negative means the charge became larger in absolute terms because outflows are negative.

description: Percent change in absolute charge amount vs. prior recurring charge.
Public-market ticker linked to the merchant, when useful.
notes: Supports the opportunity-cost or “invest instead” feature.

description: Fallback benchmark ticker for opportunity-cost comparisons.
Ticker actually purchased in a brokerage transaction.
Synthetic fractional shares purchased.
True for transfers and investment buys that should usually be excluded from spending totals.
inflow or outflow based on amount sign.
Helper note for edge cases.
Absolute value of amount.
notes: Useful for charts and price-comparison logic.

description: True for negative expense rows that should count as spending.
notes: Excludes transfers, refunds, and investment buys.

description: True on labeled rows that represent a consumer subscription price increase event.
